req.title: Consent management (boundary)
req.statement: A patient shall grant or revoke consent for data sharing; revoked consent shall be enforced within 15 minutes. Boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements must be tested.
error_pathways: accesses after revoke within SLA denied

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers consent grant/revoke lifecycle with a 15-minute SLA for cache invalidation. I selected: (1) EP positive – a valid grant then revoke sequence confirming 200 storage; (2) NEG negative – data access attempted immediately after revoke must be denied within the SLA window; (3) BVA boundary – consent start/end timestamps at exact boundary values (epoch=0, max-int seconds) and a period.duration of 0 days to probe numeric edge cases; (4) STC state-transition – full active→revoked→re-granted cycle confirming idempotent state changes; (5) DT decision-table – matrix of status × category × actor combinations to verify enforcement logic. Each test is self-contained via fixtures that create and clean up their own patient and consent resources.

tests.name: test_consent_grant_and_revoke_returns_200
preconditions: Caller holds a valid patient-scoped JWT (role:patient)
No prior consent resource exists for this patient ID
actions: POST /Consent with status=active and valid scope
Assert HTTP 201 and resource id returned
PUT /Consent/{id} with status=inactive (revoke)
Assert HTTP 200 and status=inactive in response body
expected: Grant returns HTTP 201 with resource id
trace: R-HLT-040: patient shall grant or revoke consent
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <helper_fn> import patient_token, api_client, build_consent_payload, cleanup_consent

@pytest.fixture()
def client():
    return api_client(base_url="<FHIR_BASE_URL>")

def test_consent_grant_and_revoke_returns_200(client):
    token = patient_token(patient_id="pt-ep-001")
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}

    payload = build_consent_payload(
        patient_id="pt-ep-001",
        status="active",
        category="research"
    )
    post_resp = client.post("/Consent", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert post_resp.status_code == 201
    consent_id = post_resp.json()["id"]

    revoke_payload = {**payload, "status": "inactive", "id": consent_id}
    put_resp = client.put(f"/Consent/{consent_id}", json=revoke_payload, headers=headers)
    assert put_resp.status_code == 200
    assert put_resp.json()["status"] == "inactive"

    get_resp = client.get(f"/Consent/{consent_id}", headers=headers)
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    assert get_resp.json()["status"] == "inactive"

    cleanup_consent(client, headers, consent_id)

tests.name: test_data_access_denied_after_revoke_within_sla
preconditions: Patient pt-neg-001 has an active consent already stored
Caller holds a valid patient-scoped JWT (role:patient)
actions: PUT /Consent/{id} with status=inactive to revoke consent
Immediately (< 1 s) attempt GET /Patient/pt-neg-001/$everything
Wait 14 minutes 55 seconds
Attempt GET /Patient/pt-neg-001/$everything again
Wait for SLA boundary (15 min mark) and retry
expected: Access within 15-min SLA window returns HTTP 403 or 401
Access after SLA may still return 403 (consent revoked permanently)
trace: R-HLT-040: revoked consent shall be enforced within 15 minutes
R-HLT-040: error pathway – accesses after revoke within SLA denied

tests.name: test_consent_boundary_timestamps_and_zero_duration
preconditions: Caller holds a valid patient-scoped JWT (role:patient)
actions: POST /Consent with dateTime=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z (epoch boundary)
POST /Consent with dateTime=9999-12-31T23:59:59Z (max FHIR dateTime boundary)
POST /Consent with period.duration=0 (zero-length period)
POST /Consent with period.duration=-1 (below-zero, invalid)
expected: Max dateTime accepted or server returns defined validation error <= 422
Zero-duration period returns HTTP 422 with OperationOutcome
Negative duration returns HTTP 422 with OperationOutcome
trace: R-HLT-040: boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements must be tested

tests.name: test_consent_state_transition_active_revoked_regranted
preconditions: Caller holds a valid patient-scoped JWT (role:patient)
Patient pt-stc-001 has no existing consent resource
actions: POST /Consent status=active  → state: ACTIVE
PUT  /Consent/{id} status=inactive → state: REVOKED
PUT  /Consent/{id} status=active   → state: RE-GRANTED
DELETE or PUT status=inactive to clean up
expected: Each GET after transition reflects new status
trace: R-HLT-040: patient shall grant or revoke consent
R-HLT-040: consent stored; downstream caches invalidated
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <helper_fn> import patient_token, api_client, build_consent_payload, cleanup_consent

@pytest.fixture()
def client():
    return api_client(base_url="<FHIR_BASE_URL>")

def test_consent_state_transition_active_revoked_regranted(client):
    token = patient_token(patient_id="pt-stc-001")
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}
    base = build_consent_payload(patient_id="pt-stc-001", status="active", category="treatment")

    # ACTIVE
    r1 = client.post("/Consent", json=base, headers=headers)
    assert r1.status_code == 201
    cid = r1.json()["id"]
    assert r1.json()["status"] == "active"

    # REVOKED
    r2 = client.put(f"/Consent/{cid}", json={**base, "id": cid, "status": "inactive"}, headers=headers)
    assert r2.status_code == 200
    assert r2.json()["status"] == "inactive"
    assert client.get(f"/Consent/{cid}", headers=headers).json()["status"] == "inactive"

    # RE-GRANTED
    r3 = client.put(f"/Consent/{cid}", json={**base, "id": cid, "status": "active"}, headers=headers)
    assert r3.status_code == 200
    assert r3.json()["status"] == "active"
    assert client.get(f"/Consent/{cid}", headers=headers).json()["status"] == "active"

    cleanup_consent(client, headers, cid)

tests.name: test_consent_decision_table_status_category_actor
preconditions: Non-patient (practitioner) JWT available for negative actor case
actions: POST /Consent with status=entered-in-error (invalid status) as patient → expect 422
POST /Consent with status=active, category=<unknown-code> as patient → expect 422
POST /Consent with status=active, valid category as practitioner → expect 403
POST /Consent with status=active, valid category as patient → expect 201
expected: Invalid status + valid category + patient → HTTP 422
Valid status + unknown category + patient → HTTP 422
Valid status + valid category + practitioner → HTTP 403
Valid status + valid category + patient → HTTP 201
trace: R-HLT-040: patient shall grant or revoke consent (actor constraint)
